combat_history.event: IRIS Jamaran conducted first long-range deployment to Syrian waters, demonstrating Iranian power projection capability
combat_history.significance: First deployment beyond Persian Gulf demonstrated Iranian naval ambitions but also revealed maintenance and logistics limitations

combat_history.event: IRIS Damavand lost in Caspian Sea due to weather-related accident, broke apart and sank
combat_history.significance: Highlighted structural or seaworthiness issues with Iranian indigenous construction quality

combat_history.event: IRIS Sahand participated in Iranian naval exercises amid US sanctions escalation and tanker war tensions
combat_history.significance: Demonstrated Iranian conventional naval presence during heightened regional tensions

combat_history.event: IRIS Sahand conducted extended deployment to Indian Ocean, reaching as far as Indonesia
combat_history.significance: Longest deployment demonstrated improved Iranian maintenance capabilities but also highlighted limited sustainment capacity

modernization.description: Replacement of remaining foreign components with Iranian-developed systems, improved radar and combat management systems
modernization.impact: Reduces foreign dependency but may compromise capability compared to Western systems

modernization.description: Integration of Iranian indigenous VLS for surface-to-air and surface-to-surface missiles
modernization.impact: Would significantly enhance offensive and defensive capabilities if successfully implemented

vulnerabilities.description: Loss of Damavand and reported maintenance issues suggest quality control problems in indigenous production
vulnerabilities.context: Sanctions limit access to high-grade materials and manufacturing equipment
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Increased focus on domestic quality standards, but fundamental resource constraints remain

vulnerabilities.description: Indigenous electronic systems likely vulnerable to modern Western EW capabilities and cyber warfare
vulnerabilities.context: Iranian electronics industry lacks access to cutting-edge semiconductor technology
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Development of indigenous EW capabilities, but significant technology gap remains

vulnerabilities.description: Aging SM-1 missiles and limited indigenous SAM capability provide inadequate protection against modern air threats
vulnerabilities.context: Regional adversaries possess advanced air-launched anti-ship missiles and stealth capabilities
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Development of indigenous SAM systems, but performance uncertain against modern threats

vulnerabilities.description: Limited ability to sustain extended operations due to indigenous supply chain constraints
vulnerabilities.context: Extended deployments reveal maintenance and parts availability issues
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Expansion of domestic production capacity, but resource limitations persist

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Persian Gulf patrol, occasional Indian Ocean power projection missions
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: Usually operates independently or with Iranian support vessels
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: Limited availability due to maintenance challenges and parts constraints

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Chinese frigate represents modern capabilities Iran seeks - 32 VLS cells, advanced radar, better build quality. Jamaran-class significantly less capable but represents similar indigenous development philosophy.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Sanctions vs integration - how isolation shapes naval development differently

peer_comparisons.key_differences: FREMM represents NATO-standard capabilities with 16 VLS cells, advanced sensors, and proven reliability. Jamaran-class roughly equivalent displacement but significantly less sophisticated systems.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Western vs sanctioned naval development - what $1 billion buys you

peer_comparisons.key_differences: UAE corvette smaller but with modern European systems. Represents Gulf state approach of buying Western technology vs Iranian indigenous development under sanctions.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Gulf naval arms race - buying vs building under sanctions

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Dutch design philosophy emphasizes modularity and proven systems. Iranian approach forced toward indigenous solutions with uncertain reliability and performance.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Design philosophy - proven systems vs sanctions-forced innovation
